h2: Points for language ability: maximum 24 points

sections.heading: Your score
items: Your maximum possible score is 100. The pass mark is currently 35 points, but it may change. Make sure you visit this site regularly if you’re thinking about immigrating to Canada as a self-employed immigrant.
As part of the immigration process, we may ask to interview you. During the interview, a visa officer will ask you to explain:
If there’s a difference between your statement of your qualifications and the visa officer’s assessment, we’ll award points based on the officer’s decision.
bullets: the documents that you give us
your score under each of the selection criteria
you meeting the definition of a self-employed person
information that is unclear or inconsistent
shortcomings or gaps in the documents that you provide

sections.heading: Points for experience: maximum 35 points
items: Relevant experience must have been obtained in the period that begins five years before you sign your application and ends when a decision is made on your application.

sections.heading: Points for age: maximum 10 points
items: You will be awarded selection points based on your age at the time when the visa office receives your application.

sections.heading: Points for language ability: maximum 24 points
items: Your proficiency in English or French is one of the 5 selection factors. You’ll be awarded up to 24 points for your basic, moderate or high proficiency in English and French. You’ll be given points based on your ability to:
If you have some proficiency in both English and French, decide which language you’re more comfortable using. This is your first official language. The other is your second official language.

You must prove the level of language proficiency you claim on your application if you wish to have your official language proficiency considered in the assessment of your application for permanent residence.

sections.heading: Language tests we accept
items: You can take any of these approved language tests:

sections.heading: English
bullets: CELPIP: Canadian English Language Proficiency Index Program You need to take the CELPIP-General test.
You need to take the CELPIP-General test.
IELTS: International English Language Testing System You need to take the IELTS General Training test.
You need to take the IELTS General Training test.
PTE: Pearson Test of English You need to take the PTE Core test.
You need to take the PTE Core test.

sections.heading: French
bullets: TEF Canada: Test d’évaluation de français
TCF Canada : Test de connaissance du français

sections.heading: Points for adaptability: maximum 6 points
items: A maximum of 6 points for adaptability can be earned by any combination of the following elements.
